title: senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. apulus
intro: Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. apulus, also known as Apulian Jacob's ladder or Apulo's woolly Jacob's ladder, is a subspecies of the Senecio leucanthemifolius plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. apulus include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as its potential to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, but it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedy.
